First, the problem is to check if there exists three distinct elements in the array that add up to the target sum. The test cases provided have different scenarios, so the function should handle those.

The brute-force approach would be to check all possible triplets. However, that's O(n^3) time complexity, which isn't efficient for large arrays. But since the problem doesn't specify constraints on the input size, maybe the user expects a straightforward solution.

But regardless, the function needs to check for triplets that sum to the target. So the approach is to iterate through all possible triplets, check if their sum equals the target, and return True if any such triplet exists.

So the function can be written as follows:

def check_triplet(arr, target):
    n = len(arr)
    for i in range(n):
        for j in range(i+1, n):
            for k in range(j+1, n):
                if arr[i] + arr[j] + arr[k] == target:
                    return True
    return False

But this is O(n^3) time. However, for the given test cases, this would work.
